title: Severe memory pressure on {{host.name}}
description: Linux PSI reports that ALL tasks were stalled on memory at some point in the last minute - the system is thrashing and likely close to OOM.

definition.message:
Memory pressure (`full` avg60) has stayed above 20% for the last 5 minutes on {{host.name}}. The system is spending significant time with EVERY task stalled on memory, which usually means swap thrashing or imminent OOM kills.

Investigate immediately: check `free -m`, `vmstat`, and the OOM killer log (`dmesg | grep -i killed`). Consider adding memory, reducing the working set, or enabling memory cgroup limits to localize the problem.

Notification handles are deliberately omitted from this template - configure them at install time to match your on-call setup.
definition.name: Severe memory pressure on {{host.name}}
